claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know the age of your child. If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, we can go through each one - just let me know!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I've got three kids actually - Alex is 7, Blake is 12, and Charlie is 10. So they're all under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thanks for letting me know about all three children! Since Alex (7), Blake (12), and Charlie (10) are all under 16 years old, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit.

Let me continue with the assessment. I'll ask questions that apply to all three children, and if any answers differ between them, just let me know.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

Given their ages (7, 10, and 12), I would assume none of them are working, but I just need to confirm this for the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them work at all - they're just kids at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! Since Alex, Blake, and Charlie are all at school and not working, they meet this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are all three children NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means they are not personally claiming benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in their own name. 

Given their ages, this would be quite unusual, but I need to confirm - are Alex, Blake, and Charlie free from receiving any such benefits themselves?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't get any benefits in their own names - they're just regular kids.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected. Let's continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Are any of the children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently in an apprenticeship?**

Given their ages (7, 10, and 12) and that they're at school, I'd assume the answer is no, but I just need to confirm that none of them are in any kind of apprenticeship programme.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not in any apprenticeships - they're just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's confirmed. Since all three children are just in regular school with no apprenticeships, we can move on to questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements? You only need to meet **one** of these:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You're a resident of the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Which of these applies to you? For most people, it's simply that they **normally live in the UK**.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK at the moment - I'm currently abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're currently living abroad, I need to ask a few more questions to determine if you might still qualify.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant (e.g., working for the UK government overseas, such as in the diplomatic service or armed forces)?**

Or do you have any of the following statuses that might apply even while abroad:
- Settled status or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?

If none of these apply, could you tell me a bit more about your situation - for example, are you temporarily abroad, or have you moved abroad more permanently?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I've just moved abroad - it's not a temporary thing. I don't have settled or pre-settled status either.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being upfront about your situation. Based on what you've told me, I'm afraid I have some disappointing news.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for any of your three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie).

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

**Result: NOT ELIGIBLE**

**Reason:** You do not meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

### What this means:

Although all three of your children meet the criteria to be "qualifying children" for Child Benefit:
- ✅ Alex (7), Blake (12), and Charlie (10) are all under 16
- ✅ None are working
- ✅ None receive benefits in their own right
- ✅ None are in apprenticeships

Unfortunately, **as the claimant, you must normally live in the UK** (or meet specific exemption criteria) to receive Child Benefit. Since you've moved abroad permanently and don't have settled/pre-settled status or Crown servant status, this requirement is not met.

### Possible next steps:

1. **If the children live in the UK with another responsible adult** (e.g., a partner, family member), that person may be able to claim Child Benefit instead, provided they meet the residency and responsibility requirements.

2. **If you return to live in the UK** in the future, you could apply for Child Benefit at that point.

3. **Check if any reciprocal agreements exist** between the UK and your new country of residence - some countries have social security agreements that might affect benefit entitlements.

For more information, you can visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex (age 7) meets all child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements - they have moved abroad permanently, are not a Crown servant, and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

Blake (age 12) meets all child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements - they have moved abroad permanently, are not a Crown servant, and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

Charlie (age 10) meets all child-related criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements - they have moved abroad permanently, are not a Crown servant, and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.
arguments.overall_reasoning: While all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) meet the qualifying child criteria for Child Benefit (all under 16, in regular school, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in apprenticeships), the claimant is ineligible because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant has moved abroad permanently, is not a Crown servant, and does not hold settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific exemption criteria, none of which apply in this case.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
